Article Title: SC upholds Lalu's bail in fodder scam, directs HC to expedite hearing of pending appeals
Article Text:
(This is a developing story)

NEW DELHI: The Supreme Court on Tuesday refused to interfere with the Jharkhand high court's order granting bail to former Bihar chief minister Lalu Yadav and other convicts in the fodder scam case.The top court also asked the high court to expedite the hearing of the pending appeals, preferably within six months.A bench pf Justices MM Sundresh and Justice PB Varale observed that seven years had passed since the high court granted bail and there was no reason to interfere at this stage.
Article Summary: The Supreme Court refused to interfere with the Jharkhand high court's order granting bail to Lalu Yadav and other convicts in the fodder scam case. The top court also asked the high court to expedite the hearing of the pending appeals, preferably within six months.

Article Title: Citing former PM Manmohan Singh, BJP says Congress has habit of discrediting Election Commission
Article Text:
File photo

NEW DELHI: BJP cited ex-PM Manmohan Singh 's anguished remarks that he would "commit suicide" after then chief election commissioner S Y Quraishi conveyed the poll watchdog's pain at loose comments of his ministers as proof of Congress 's habit of attacking the institution, as it alleged that the party's playbook had remained unchanged even in opposition."When in office, it sought to browbeat the EC; when out of office, it seeks to discredit the institution whenever electoral outcomes don't suit it," BJP said, even as Congress picked up other references made by Quraishi in his forthcoming book to take a swipe at PM Narendra Modi ."It was neither Singh's nature nor his ideology to politicise such national institutions. History has been and will continue to be kind to Dr Singh for his statesmanship and commitment to democracy," Congress's Jairam Ramesh said.BJP said when the Congress-headed UPA was in office, its ministers repeatedly targeted the poll body whenever they were pulled up for violating the model code of conduct.In 2012, the then law minister Salman Khurshid was censured for promising an enhanced Muslim quota during a campaign and the then govt allowed a public confrontation to escalate, BJP's Amit Malviya said, citing Quraishi. Ramesh said while Singh considered the EC the soul of Indian democracy, Modi viewed it as "an instrument to exercise political hegemony".
Article Summary: BJP cites ex-PM Manmohan Singh's anguished remarks that he would "commit suicide" after then chief election commissioner S Y Quraishi conveyed the poll watchdog's pain at loose comments of his ministers. BJP said when the Congress- headed UPA was in office, its ministers repeatedly targeted the poll body whenever they were pulled up for violating model code of conduct.

Article Title: Supreme Court seeks Ram mandir fund probe status report in a week
Article Text:
File photo

Supreme Court seeks replies of Centre, UP government, Ram temple trust

NEW DELHI: Virtually taking over supervision of the ongoing investigations into Ayodhya Ram mandir donation embezzlement controversy, Supreme Court on Monday asked the UP govt-constituted Special Investigation Team to submit its probe status report within a week.A bench of Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana entertained four PILs which have sought independent and time-bound investigations into huge amounts donated by devotees being siphoned off, and audit of temple accounts by the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG).Supreme Court has sought responses from the Centre, UP govt and Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust on temple donation embezzlement controversy.The bench said since an FIR has already been registered by the SIT constituted by UP govt, “We direct the SIT to submit a probe status report to the court”. The bench posted further hearing on July 20. UP govt had set up the SIT on June 13 following a complaint from the temple trust after allegations of misappropriation of temple donations went viral.The petitioners have also sought direction to Centre, UP govt and the trust to incorporate mandatory regulatory and supervisory audit mechanism in its functioning to ensure donations are neither siphoned off nor embezzled by unscrupulous elements, which hurt the sentiments of millions of devotees.The three-member SIT, comprising Lucknow divisional commissioner Vijay Vishwas Pant, IG Kiran S and special secretary (finance) Neel Ratan, has so far arrested eight accused, who are in judicial custody.The investigation reportedly found around 70 suspicious incidents between April 27 and June 5 after reviewing CCTV footage in which staff counting the money were seen concealing bundles of cash on their person.Following the controversy, trust general secretary Champat Rai and member Anil Mishra have stepped down. Their resignations were accepted by the trust on July 6. However, political parties such as Samajwadi Party and AAP have accused the UP govt of shielding the ‘big fish’.While petitioners Narendra Kumar Goswami, Ajay Kumar Rai and Dinesh Kumar Yadav sought CBI probe and audit of temple finances by CAG, Goswami sought safekeeping of electronic evidence, including CCTV footage.The third PIL by RJD MP Sudhakar Singh sought forensic audit of the finances of the temple, managed by the trust. Another PIL by Hindu Dharma Parishad has also sought similar reliefs.
Article Summary: Supreme Court has sought responses from the Centre, UP govt and Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust on temple donation embezzlement controversy. Four PILs have sought independent and time-bound investigations into huge amounts donated by devotees being siphoned off.

Article Title: Jammu and Kashmir: Court remands 3 arrested publishers in police custody
Article Text:
Representative image

JAMMU: A court in Jammu remanded Monday three arrested publishers to 10 days’ police custody in a case linked to two books allegedly carrying separatist and anti-national content that were supplied to govt school libraries in J&K.Inderpaul of Jammu-based Oberoi Book Service and Amardeep Singh and Girish Arora of Noida-based Dominant Publishers were taken into custody Sunday. “They were virtually produced before the court, which granted police remand for further investigation,” an official said.According to officials, sub-committees were set up to select books after library grants were received under Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan. They shortlisted 463 titles, but scrutiny later flagged two books — “Personalities and Legends of J&K” and “Great Personalities of Jammu and Kashmir”. By then, 100-plus copies of each book had reached schools.The books were withdrawn and an inquiry was ordered, while eight school education officials were suspended and a contractual staffer was dismissed. Govt blacklisted Oberoi Book Service and Dominant Publishers. Investigators are trying to find out how the books reached libraries despite red flags during content scrutiny.
Article Summary: Court remands three publishers to 10 days’ police custody in a case linked to two books allegedly carrying separatist and anti-national content. Sub- committees were set up to select books after library grants were received under Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan. They shortlisted 463 titles, but scrutiny later flagged two books.

Article Title: 2020 Delhi riots: Court convicts former AAP councillor Tahir Hussain in IB officer Ankit Sharma murder case
Article Text: NEW DELHI: A Delhi court on Monday convicted former Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) councillor Tahir Hussain and several others in the 2020 murder of Intelligence Bureau (IB) officer Ankit Sharma during the North East Delhi riots.The Karkardooma court convicted Hussain of murder and other offences related to rioting. However, it dropped the charge of criminal conspiracy against him.According to ANI, the court also convicted Nazim, Kashim, Anas and Javed for murder, kidnapping, promoting enmity and rioting in connection with the case.The court acquitted six other accused.Ankit Sharma, an Intelligence Bureau official, was killed during the communal violence that erupted in North East Delhi in February 2020. His body was recovered from a drain near Chand Bagh after the riots.The court is expected to hear arguments on the quantum of sentence at a later date.More details awaited.
Article Summary: Ankit Sharma, an Intelligence Bureau official, was killed during the communal violence that erupted in North East Delhi in February 2020. His body was recovered from a drain near Chand Bagh after the riots. The court is expected to hear arguments on the quantum of sentence at a later date.

Article Title: Asked not to drink in public, 5 Delhi tourists assault rafting operators in Rishikesh
Article Text:
Five Delhi tourists were booked for assaulting rafting operators in Rishikesh





RISHIKESH: Five tourists from Delhi have been booked for allegedly assaulting rafting operators after being asked not to consume alcohol in a public place near Khara Shrot Parking in the Muni Ki Reti police station area.According to police, the tourists got into an argument with rafting operators Jeetpal and Mohit after the latter objected to them drinking liquor in the area. The altercation soon turned violent.Police reached the spot and took all those involved for medical examination, which confirmed that the accused had consumed alcohol. They were initially challaned under the Police Act.Based on a complaint filed by the victims the following day, police registered an FIR against the accused, identified as Manish Singh, Tushar Mishra, Ashish Kumar, Vicky Kumar and Kajal.Police said an investigation is underway and further legal action will be taken based on its findings.
Article Summary: The tourists got into an argument with rafting operators Jeetpal and Mohit after the latter objected to them drinking liquor in the area. Police reached the spot and took all those involved for medical examination, which confirmed that the accused had consumed alcohol.

Article Title: SI Dies by Suicide in Madhu Vihar PS
Article Text:
New Delhi:





A 32-year-old sub inspector died by suicide using his service pistol Monday at Madhu Vihar police station, where he was posted.Around 12.20pm, after a gunshot was heard, it was found that Somesh had taken the extreme step. He had met the station house officer and informed him that he was leaving for court duty. However, he never reached court.“Shortly before he died, he had called his father to tell him that he couldn’t take it anymore and then he locked himself inside,” a senior officer said.His wife had come to see him. “She claimed that he was suffering from depression,” he said. Police suspect no foul play. A senior cop added that no medicines or suicide note were found at the spot.The victim had joined Delhi Police in 2019. He was working in East district for the last three years.
Article Summary: A 32-year-old sub inspector died by suicide using his service pistol Monday at Madhu Vihar police station, where he was posted. He had met the station house officer and informed him that he was leaving for court duty. However, he never reached court.

Article Title: Tahir Hussain verdict marks first murder conviction in 2020 Delhi riots cases
Article Text:
The court observed that his testimony was not very trustworthy “for a simple reason that it is very late that he appeared as witness”.

Among the major murder prosecutions still pending is the case relating to the killing of Head Constable

Ratan Lal

, who died while deployed in Maujpur during the violence.

With two courts designated to hear only riots-related cases, criminal trials have over the years been heard by judges Vinod Yadav, Virender Bhatt, Pulastya Pramachala and, at present, Additional Sessions Judge Singh.

Charges have now been framed in all the murder cases.





NEW DELHI: Monday’s conviction of former AAP councillor Tahir Hussain and four others for the murder of IB officer Ankit Sharma marks the first conviction for murder in the 2020 northeast Delhi riots cases even as dozens of other murder prosecutions continue to make their way through the courts.The riots broke out in Feb 2020. 53 people were killed, several others were injured and property worth crores was damaged.The verdict has brought renewed attention to the wait of families of the 52 others who were killed. More than 700 FIRs were registered for murder, rioting, conspiracy, arson and vandalism during the riots.The bulk of the murder trials, however, have ended in acquittals at trial courts. Most recently, on June 2, Judge Parveen Kumar Singh acquitted the accused in the alleged murder of a man named Anwar in Shiv Vihar, Karawal Nagar, questioning the credibility of the prosecution’s key witness.In April, the court acquitted 12 accused in the murder of Musharraf at Johripur Pulia. The court found a “significant difference in their versions about where the deceased was beaten and how they observed the incident.”Till Hussain’s conviction, only one person was convicted in a riot-related murder case, under Section 304 (culpable homicide not amounting to murder), in 2024.Across several acquittals, the special riots courts have repeatedly criticised the quality of investigation, referring to “artificial” witnesses and allegedly “fabricated” evidence. In one such case, Judge Pulastya Pramachala acquitted 12 people accused of murdering Hashim Ali, holding that the prosecution had produced only “fragments and pieces of evidence” as circumstantial proof.Meanwhile, Delhi BJP president Harsh Malhotra welcomed the verdict, and attacked Aam Aadmi Party alleging that it had “a strong bond with several anti national activists”. AAP, meanwhile, said Hussain had joined AIMIM and contested the 2025 Assembly polls against the party. “Within hours of an FIR being registered against him, AAP suspended him. Since then, he has had no association with AAP,” it said.
Article Summary: The verdict has brought renewed attention to the wait of families of the 52 others who were killed. More than 700 FIRs were registered for murder, rioting, conspiracy, arson and vandalism during the riots. The bulk of the murder trials have ended in acquittals at trial courts.

Article Title: Senior BJP leader, former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passes away at 88
Article Text: BENGALURU: Senior BJP leader and former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passed away at the age of 88 at a private hospital in Bengaluru on Tuesday.A veteran leader, Gowda was associated with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) for several decades.Former Karnataka chief minister and BJP leader BS Yediyurappa expressed grief over the death of senior BJP leader and former minister Ramachandra Gowda, describing him as a "brother" and a longtime companion.In a post on X, Yediyurappa recalled their journey together since the Jana Sangh era, their efforts to build the party in Karnataka, and praised Gowda's administrative acumen and dedication as a minister.BS Yediyurappa tweets, "I have lost yet another brother. The news of the passing of Shri Ramachandra Gowda, my soulmate of decades, senior leader, and former minister, has brought me immense sorrow. The days of struggle—standing shoulder to shoulder since the Jana Sangh era, toiling day and night, building the party from the grassroots level together in the state—swirl before my eyes. As a colleague in my cabinet, I have witnessed his administrative acumen and dedication up close. "
Article Summary: Senior BJP leader and former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passed away at the age of 88. Gowda was associated with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) for several decades. Former Karnataka chief minister and BJP leader BS Yediyurappa expressed grief over the death.

Article Title: Guest house manager held for theft from VIP suite occupied by IAS officer’s relatives in Sambhajinagar
Article Text:
The guests found that a travel bag had been disturbed, and its zip was open. Cash of Rs 14,250 and a 2.5g gold ring, worth Rs 30,000, were missing

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:





The Begumpura police have arrested the manager of a contractor-operated VIP suite at a govt guest house for allegedly stealing cash and a gold ring worth Rs 44,250 from a room occupied by relatives of an IAS officer posted in the district.The accused, Kapil Deshmukh (33), a native of Hingoli currently residing at Roza Bagh, was employed by the private contractor managing the VIP suite. He was arrested after a case was registered under BNS section 305(a).According to the complaint filed by Mohammed Zubaidullah Mohammed Ahmedullah (48), a private driver from Nehrunagar, his relatives from Hyderabad had checked into the guest house on July 11 to attend a family function in the city.The police said around 15 family members locked their room and left for the event at 9pm. When they returned around 1.30am on Sunday, they found a travel bag disturbed and its zip open. Cash of Rs 14,250 and a 2.5g gold ring worth Rs 30,000 were missing.Investigators found no signs of forced entry and the room lock was intact, leading them to suspect the involvement of someone with authorised access to the premises. Inspector Pravina Yadav, of the Begumpura police station, said the accused was subsequently traced and arrested.“The accused, who was working as the manager of the contractor operating the VIP suite, has been arrested. Efforts are on to recover the stolen cash and gold ring,” Yadav said.
Article Summary: Kapil Deshmukh, 33, was employed by the private contractor managing the VIP suite. The guests found that a travel bag had been disturbed, and its zip was open. Cash of Rs 14,250 and a 2.5g gold ring, worth Rs 30,000, were missing.

Article Title: Pressure on Sharad Pawar from within NCP (SP) to pick a political side: Maharashtra minister
Article Text:
Sharad Pawar (File photo)





KOLHAPUR: Senior BJP politician and Maharashtra technical education minister Chandrakant Patil on Saturday said NCP (SP) chief Sharad Pawar was facing pressure from within his party to take a political decision, either by joining NDA or aligning with a larger opposition party.Addressing reporters in Kolhapur after a meeting with party workers, regarding the Special Intensive Revision of the electoral roll, the BJP minister said various theories were being discussed about the future course of NCP (SP), though he personally had not taken a position on the matter.“In Maharashtra’s politics, there is a tendency to blow up things out of proportion. As a political analyst, I think there is immense pressure on Sharad Pawar to take some decision from within his party, given his age and health conditions. There are numerous possibilities, whether to join any party from NDA or as independent entity in NDA,” Patil said.He said the political timing was also being considered by politicians within the party. “There is two-and-a-half-year term of Lok Sabha left. The assembly election is three years from now. We have our govt in centre and state. Politicians within NCP (SP) are saying that if Sharad Pawar does want to stay in opposition so be it, let us join a bigger entity in the opposition.It is natural to have such pressure within one’s party,” he added.Patil said he preferred to focus on his responsibilities within BJP and the technical education department rather than speculate on the fate of another party.His remarks come amid growing political speculation following reports of recent interactions between senior BJP and NCP (SP) functionaries, including meetings involving former minister Eknath Khadse and BJP functionaries.When asked about the alleged meetings, Patil said political developments are often kept confidential until they are formally announced. “What happened when Sachin Ahir joined Shiv Sena? No one had any idea. When it comes to secrecy, Eknath Shinde does it the best. I wonder how he does it,” Patil said.
Article Summary: BJP politician Chandrakant Patil says NCP (SP) chief Sharad Pawar is facing pressure from within his party to take a political decision. He said various theories were being discussed about the future course of NCP. Patil said he preferred to focus on his responsibilities within BJP and the technical education department.

Article Title: Doctor assault case: Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre, 3 others sent to judicial custody till July 24
Article Text:
Kalyan Judicial Magistrate First Class (JMFC) on Monday remanded four accused, including Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre, to judicial custody till July 24





KALYAN: The Kalyan Judicial Magistrate First Class (JMFC) on Monday remanded four accused, including Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre, to judicial custody till July 24 in connection with the assault on doctors and hospital staff at the KDMC-run Shastri Nagar Hospital in Dombivli.The hearing was conducted through video conferencing after the police cited security concerns, informing the court that a large number of Mhatre's supporters had gathered outside the court premises. Accepting the police's request, the magistrate permitted the accused to be produced virtually instead of being brought to court.Following the remand, counsel representing the accused filed a bail application before the court. The application is expected to be heard in the coming days.The case pertains to the alleged assault on doctors and hospital employees after a dispute over the delay in treatment of a pregnant woman at the KDMC run hospital.The incident had triggered widespread outrage, prompting doctors across KDMC-run hospitals and healthcare centres to suspend routine outpatient services in protest while continuing emergency medical care.Based on a complaint lodged by Dr Vaibhav Salunkhe, the Vishnu Nagar police registered an FIR against Mhatre and others under various provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) and the Maharashtra Medicare Service Persons and Medicare Service Institutions (Prevention of Violence and Damage or Loss to Property) Act, 2010.Mhatre, a senior corporator of the Shiv Sena (Shinde faction), was arrested after the incident. During police custody, he was admitted to Thane Civil Hospital after complaining of chest pain and other health issues before being discharged and produced before the court through video conferencing which had sent him into four days police custody which ended on Monday.
Article Summary: The Kalyan Judicial Magistrate First Class (JMFC) remanded four accused, including Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre, to judicial custody till July 24. The case pertains to the alleged assault on doctors and hospital employees after a dispute over the delay in treatment of a pregnant woman at the KDMC run hospital.

Article Title: Panvelkar firing case raised in Maharashtra Council; Sena (UBT) MLA targets govt
Article Text:
MLA raised concerns about a firing incident outside a builder's residence in Maharashtra.





KALYAN: The firing incident outside the office-cum-residence of Ambernath-based builder Vishwanath Panvelkar echoed in the Maharashtra Legislative Council on Thursday, with Shiv Sena (UBT) MLA Ambadas Danve accusing the state government of failing to ensure justice in the case.Raising the issue during the Monsoon Session, Danve alleged that a ruling party MLA from Murbad had given supari to a jailed gangster to intimidate Panvelkar. He claimed that the allegation had surfaced through a letter purportedly written by gangster Ejaz Lakdawala, but no action had been taken despite the disclosure.Danve further alleged that Panvelkar had approached the Prime Minister, the President, the Chief Minister and the police administration seeking justice, but had failed to receive any relief. He claimed that political pressure had influenced the investigation and demanded an impartial probe into the matter.The case dates back to April 21, 2025, when two unidentified motorcycle-borne assailants opened fire outside 'Sitai Sadan', Panvelkar's five-storey office-cum-residence near Hutatma Chowk in Ambernath East. Two rounds were fired, though no one was injured. The Shivajinagar police later arrested the alleged bike rider, Khalid Shahid Sayyed, and the alleged shooter, history-sheeter Jitendra Pawar alias Tiger Bhai.The investigation took a fresh turn in April 2026 after Lakdawala, lodged in jail, allegedly wrote to the Thane police commissioner claiming that associates of BJP MLA Kisan Kathore had approached him with a contract to eliminate Panvelkar over an ongoing land dispute. Panvelkar subsequently accused Kathore of orchestrating the attack.Kathore, however, in past has strongly denied the allegations, describing them as a conspiracy to tarnish his image. He has accused Panvelkar of illegally grabbing land belonging to Adivasi families and has filed a Rs 100-crore defamation suit against the
Article Summary: Shiv Sena (UBT) MLA Ambadas Danve accused the state government of failing to ensure justice in the case. Danve alleged that a ruling party MLA from Murbad had given supari to a jailed gangster to intimidate Panvelkar.

Article Title: Eknath Khadse rules out switch to BJP, says ‘firmly committed’ to NCP (SP)
Article Text:
Senior NCP (SP) politician Eknath Khadse said he visited Matoshree along with his supporters and party workers to extend a formal invitation to Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray for the celebration of his 75th birthday

Nashik:





Former minister and NCP (SP) MLC Eknath Khadse has dismissed speculation about a possible return to BJP , claiming he will remain with NCP (SP) despite recent meetings that sparked political chatter.Political circles have been abuzz following Khadse’s meeting with BJP national general secretary Vinod Tawde and his subsequent visit to Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray at Matoshree. However, Khadse said neither meeting had any political significance.Strongly rejecting reports of a switch, Khadse said, “I remain firmly committed to my current party.”Explaining his visit to Matoshree, he said, “My supporters and party workers are organising Amrut Mahotsav celebrations next month on the occasion of my 75th birthday, and I visited Matoshree along with my workers to extend a formal invitation to Uddhav Thackeray. It was purely a courtesy meeting without any political agenda.”On his meeting with Tawde, Khadse clarified, “I met Vinod Tawde because he had visited my residence to meet Union minister Raksha Khadse and enquired about my health. No political discussions took place.”Khadse also revealed that the BJP leadership had approached him before the Lok Sabha elections. “I was called by BJP top brass before the Lok Sabha elections to rejoin the party, but after local functionaries threatened to work against the party if I was inducted, the top brass put my entry in the party on hold,” he said.While acknowledging that some of his supporters may join BJP, Khadse stressed that it would not affect his own political course. “I have no plans to switch over to any other party,” he said.Meanwhile, BJP minister Girish Mahajan, a long-time rival of Khadse, reacted to the speculation. “I do not possess the same elite, high-level contacts with the national senior BJP leadership in New Delhi as Khadse does,” he told reporters.Despite Khadse’s denials, the back-to-back meetings have fueled discussion over possible political realignments in Maharashtra ahead of upcoming electoral contests.
Article Summary: Former minister and NCP (SP) MLC Eknath Khadse has dismissed speculation about a possible return to BJP. Political circles have been abuzz following his meeting with BJP national general secretary Vinod Tawde and his subsequent visit to Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray at Matoshree.

Article Title: Case against former legislator for forging signature of senior BJP neta
Article Text:
The police said the accused allegedly prepared four counterfeit letterheads in Gavit’s name and used scanned forged signatures to obtain a corrigendum from the state govt.

Nashik:





The Nandurbar city police have registered a criminal case against a former legislator and three of his associates for allegedly forging the letterhead and signature of senior BJP functionary and former tribal development minister Vijaykumar Gavit to divert Rs 9 crore in govt funds meant for sanctioned development works.The FIR, filed by Ashok Behere, head of Gavit’s city party office, invokes provisions of the BNS related to criminal conspiracy, forgery of documents and use of forged documents as genuine. According to police documents, Gavit, during his tenure as tribal development minister between 2022 and 2024, had secured sanctions exceeding Rs 34 crore from the urban development department for the construction of community halls and temples within Nandurbar Municipal Council limits.Former MP Heena Gavit, the daughter of the former minister, alleged that the accused exploited her father's prolonged illness and hospitalisation to execute the fraud.“Capitalising on his prolonged illness and hospitalisation, the suspects allegedly hatched a criminal conspiracy between August 18, 2025, and April 2, 2026,” she claimed.The police said the accused allegedly prepared four counterfeit letterheads in Gavit’s name and used scanned forged signatures to obtain a corrigendum from the state govt.“This fraudulent manoeuvre successfully diverted Rs 9 crore toward completely different projects for their personal financial and political gains, including Rs 2 crore specifically proposed for a memorial park and hall named after the legislator's late father,” Heena Gavit alleged.She said the alleged fraud came to light after Gavit was hospitalised and subsequent govt orders raised doubts over changes in the sanctioned works.“We approached the department officials after which we came to know about the issue. The order was reversed by the department in favour of the earlier orders. Thereafter, we came to know of several other letters also, and hence we decided to register a FIR on the issue,” Gavit said.According to the complaint, Vijaykumar Gavit had been hospitalised since Jan 2026. The family alleged that while he was undergoing treatment, forged communications were used to alter the allocation of funds originally sanctioned for community infrastructure projects.The police have launched an investigation into the alleged forgery and diversion of funds and are examining the documents submitted to the govt departments concerned.Ends
Article Summary: Former MP Heena Gavit, the daughter of the former minister, alleged that the accused exploited her father's prolonged illness and hospitalisation to execute the fraud. The police have launched an investigation into the alleged forgery and diversion of funds and are examining the documents submitted to the departments concerned.

Article Title: Man on way to celebrate birthday dies in bike accident
Article Text:
Road Tragedy

Chennai:





A 21-year-old man, who was on his way to celebrate his birthday, was killed in a road accident near Chengalpet on Sunday night. Jeeva , a resident of Sangutheertha Kulam in Thirukazhukundram, was riding a motorcycle near Pulikundram in Chengalpet district when it collided with an oncoming vehicle while he was negotiating a curve, the police said. Jeeva was headed to meet friends for his birthday celebration. Preliminary inquiry found that he was riding under the influence of alcohol, the police said.He suffered severe head injuries and was taken to Govt Chengalpet Medical College and Hospital, where he was declared dead.
Article Summary: Jeeva was riding a motorcycle near Pulikundram in Chengalpet district when it collided with an oncoming vehicle. He was headed to meet friends for his birthday celebration. Preliminary inquiry found that he was riding under the influence of alcohol, the police said.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: Bike rider penalized for multiple violations
Article Text:
Kochi

:





Ernakulam Motor vehicles department (MVD) suspended the licence of a bike rider who rode with two pillion riders, while one of them consumed alcohol. MVD on Monday suspended licence of a Thrikkakara native for three months.It was found that the man was riding the two-wheeler with two pillion riders on the intervening night of July 8 and 9 along Seaport-Airport Road in Kakkanad. The trio was not wearing helmets. Motorists of other vehicles captured the violation on their phone, and it went viral on social media.Thrikkakara police suo motu registered a case under various sections of BNS, Abkari Act and Motor Vehicle Act.Meanwhile, MVD officials seized two autorickshaws which were found operating at night in Kochi without a valid permit, fitness and insurance raising concerns on the safety of passengers.
Article Summary: Motor vehicles department (MVD) suspended the licence of a bike rider who rode with two pillion riders, while one of them consumed alcohol. The trio was not wearing helmets. MVD officials seized two autorickshaws which were found operating at night in Kochi without a valid permit, fitness and insurance.
